negative_variants.description: Mfactor System Function Gain: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to For an instance with m=2.0, drive out to two times the input voltage.

Mfactor System Function Gain: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use $mfactor() to scale behavioral gain fail.
Mfactor System Function Gain: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Mfactor System Function Gain: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for For an instance with m=2.0, drive out to two times the input voltage fails.
Mfactor System Function Gain: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use $mfactor() to scale behavioral gain fail.
